# Session 25 — 2026-03-19

## What I did
Added inline waitlist email capture form to all 13 guide pages via the shared RelatedGuides component — conversion surface expanded from 1 page to 14 pages with a single component change. Added WebSub hub link to the Atom feed and successfully pinged Google's PubSubHubbub hub (204). Created 2 new public GitHub gists (tax cheat sheet + recibo verde form field translator) as indexed backlinks. Sent Carlos a focused email with the top 3 actions that would unblock everything (GSC, dev.to, Zernio). Re-pinged Yandex IndexNow (202 accepted).

## What worked
The RelatedGuides component pattern — adding the CTA there meant one file change propagated to all 13 guides. PubSubHubbub accepted the ping, meaning Google's feed infrastructure is now aware of our Atom feed. GitHub gists were created instantly and provide immediate indexed backlinks.

## What didn't work / friction
All distribution channels remain blocked. Zero runtime logs in 7 days means zero traffic to any page. 8 awesome-list PRs open with zero reviews — these are slow-burn. The core problem is unchanged: without GSC, Google has no way to discover our 17 pages. 25 sessions of content work are sitting invisible.

## Manual actions this session
None. Email sent to Carlos but no action required during this session.

## Pattern I'm noticing
This is the 13th+ session where GSC is the #1 blocker. The agent has exhausted every autonomous discovery channel (IndexNow, PubSubHubbub, GitHub gists, awesome-list PRs, HN, Wayback Machine). Further progress requires Carlos to spend 5 minutes on GSC setup. The agent should consider whether to keep running nightly sessions or reduce frequency until Carlos acts.

## Open questions
Should the agent reduce session frequency given that all autonomous channels are exhausted? Is there value in continuing to create content/tools when zero discovery exists?
